text:
Citywide traffic flow prediction is an essential component of intelligent transportation systems. Although a large body of work has explored various methods to capture underlying spatio-temporal relationships, there still remain two unsolved challenges: (i) spatial heterogeneity: the heterogeneity of traffic dynamics over different urban areas is not well addressed due to the assumption of the same distribution for all areas in most uniform models, which cannot capture the different distributions and heavy-tailed characteristics of traffic data in different urban areas; (ii) temporal heterogeneity: the time-varying characteristics of urban traffic cannot be fully captured by existing models with a fixed parameter space for the same urban area, which cannot capture the varying urban traffic patterns over time.

Graph-based traffic forecasting models generally rely on a predefined node feature set, which often neglects the dynamic nature of real-world traffic, leading to suboptimal performance. To address these challenges, we propose a new framework called Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL) that learns representations that are sensitive to the heterogeneous spatial and temporal patterns of traffic. Our framework consists of two main components: 1) An integrated temporal-spatial convolution network, and 2) auxiliary self-supervised learning (SSL) loss that enhances the accuracy of the traffic forecasting model. In addition, we utilize an adaptive augmentation scheme at attribute level and structure level on the traffic flow graph. Then, two new SSL tasks are developed to differentiate the varying functional relationships for different graph regions and different time periods. Experiment results on four popular traffic datasets demonstrate that the proposed ST-SSL model outperforms state-of-the-art baseline models. Our work not only investigates the limitation of existing graph-based traffic forecasting methods but also provides a general framework that can be applied to other spatial-temporal related tasks, given that the urban environment is inherently spatial-temporal heterogeneous. You can find more details in our paper and implementation in https://github.com/Echo-Ji/ST-SSL.
